title: Requisition 1A — N/S corridor strap
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-A: N/S AXIS ALIGNMENT
body: SEC-082 N/S node latency exceeds municipal flow audit tolerance. East/West ILC-92 heads are strapped out of service for this docket. Establish baseline hourly discharge on the active axis only.
bullets: Discharge quota: 150 units from sector tally.
Maintain N/S mast priority on the lamp bus.
E/W approaches read open-circuit for this installation site.
bureauMemo: Mayoral district memo: East Street audit window. Hold zero chronic queue dwell on EAST_STRAIGHT (movement 0x0B) during published peak slice or Budget Office opens Form B-19 inquiry.

title: Requisition 1B — E/W corridor strap
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-B: E/W AXIS ALIGNMENT
body: N/S ILC-92 returns are masked for diagnostic burn-in. East/West upstream buffers are backing into the industrial district. Restore mandated discharge without exceeding the thermal duty envelope on the relay shelf.
bullets: Discharge quota: 250 units from sector tally.
Document minimum discharge on both E and W mast bundles.
Liability: buffer saturation (ERROR_0x94) posts an incident record.
Keep E/W movements electrically isolated across macro-slices per Form 12-C.
bureauMemo: Treasury strap: E/W corridor procurement is pre-capped; extra phase banks bill as capital equipment, not O&M. Legal will not backfill unapproved relay shelves.

title: Requisition 1C — Full compass docket
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-C: FULL OPERATION
body: Diagnostics closed. All ILC-92 heads on SEC-082 are live. Upstream registers show critical depth. Author a four-bank phase-sequence image that services every strapped approach. Thermal duty sum must remain inside the published synchronization envelope.
bullets: Discharge quota: 250 units from sector tally.
Each compass shall log minimum discharge per Form 7-B addendum.
Liability: buffer saturation (ERROR_0x94) posts an incident record.
Partition incompatible .GO pairs into separate relay slices.
bureauMemo: Commissioner nephew holds parcel on North approach; auditors watch NORTH_LEFT dwell. Any ILC spike over 12 units without relief slice triggers Facilities review.

title: Requisition 1D — Protected turn banks
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-D: LEFT TURN CONFLICT
body: Field telemetry shows northbound units failing permissive merge against southbound through discharge. Install dedicated protected-left relay slices so N_LEFT and S_LEFT never share the conflict plane with opposing through vectors on the same macro-slice.
bullets: Discharge quota: 200 units from sector tally.
N_LEFT and S_LEFT each receive bank time isolated from opposing through.
Kinetic overlap (ERROR_0x82) voids warranty; design for annunciator silence.
initialCode:
phase(1):
    NORTH_STRAIGHT.GO
    SOUTH_STRAIGHT.GO

phase(2):
    # Add Left Turn Logic here
bureauMemo: Protected-turn carve-out is statutory; opposing through vectors must not occupy the same conflict plane macro-slice as N_LEFT / S_LEFT per OGAS 1.4 fault class notes.

title: Requisition 2A — Asymmetric ingress ROM
subject: DIRECTIVE 83-A: ASYMMETRIC LOAD
body: Morning peak fused into approach ROM: northbound demand index is triple the other axes. Re-time relay banks so the heavy mast meets discharge quota without starving southbound ILC returns below audit minimums.
bullets: Discharge quota: 300 units from sector tally.
Prioritize northbound bank duty within thermal envelope.
Southbound minimum discharge remains binding on the docket.
bureauMemo: Asymmetric ROM is sealed; tampering with N-demand weight voids the corridor waiver. Southbound minimum discharge remains a binding audit vector.

title: Requisition 2B — Thru-lane fuse blow
subject: DIRECTIVE 83-B: THRU-LANE CLOSURE
body: Utility cutover has blown the east/west through-lane fuses on the as-built schematic. Only left and right vectors are electrically present; discharge must route via turning movements and side-street spill paths.
bullets: Discharge quota: 200 units from sector tally.
E/W mast: LEFT and RIGHT only; THRU reads open-circuit.
Buffer saturation risk is elevated — monitor ILC-92 returns.
bureauMemo: Utility cites fuse blow on E/W thru lanes; detour signage is political. Document every turning-phase dwell for Council packet 17-U.

title: Requisition 2C — SOR-Walk mandate window
subject: DIRECTIVE 83-C: CROSSWALK PRIORITY
body: School-dismissal window is fused into corridor ROM. Pedestrian volume requires a dedicated SOR-Walk slice: System Override 0x1A tri-states all vehicular vectors while crosswalk IDs 0x0D–0x10 assert. Municipal flow audit still applies once discharge resumes.
bullets: Discharge quota: 250 units from sector tally.
Assert EXCLUSIVE_PEDESTRIAN_PHASE.GO per Mandate 44-B.
Minimize municipal flow audit inflation from override dwell.
bureauMemo: SOR-Walk mandate is clocked; extended vehicular green during override posts to School Board docket. Keep override macro-slices short on paper.

title: Requisition 3A — PLGA permissive merge
subject: DIRECTIVE 84-A: THE YIELD PROTOCOL
body: Node efficiency audit is below target. Where geometry permits, arm permissive .YIELD on turning vectors so PLGA gap logic negotiates discharge against streams already granted green-compatible lamps, instead of isolating every conflict in extra relay banks.
bullets: Discharge quota: 300 units from sector tally.
Minimum two movements shall use .YIELD on the lamp bus.
Reduce approach chronograph wait without tripping thermal duty.
bureauMemo: PLGA gap logic is authorized only where geometry yields non-zero lateral clearance; do not cite revoked Operator Miller EEPROM dumps in incident defense.

title: Requisition 3B — ILC-92 comparator install
subject: DIRECTIVE 84-B: DYNAMIC ROUTING
body: Field kit now includes additional Physical Sensor Head Requisitions. Author comparator arms (if (QUEUE…)) that clock phase_insert micro-slices only when the ILC-92 return exceeds your literal threshold — each arm bills against corridor BOM.
bullets: Discharge quota: 350 units from sector tally.
Deploy at least one QUEUE comparator + phase_insert pair (e.g. NORTH_LEFT depth).
Limit relay bank count to keep thermal duty inside envelope.
initialCode:
phase(1):
    NORTH_STRAIGHT.GO
    SOUTH_STRAIGHT.GO

phase(2):
    EAST_STRAIGHT.GO
    WEST_STRAIGHT.GO

if (QUEUE.NORTH_LEFT > 5):
    phase_insert(NORTH_LEFT.GO)
bureauMemo: Each QUEUE comparator arm bills as Class-B sensor head; Procurement caps this corridor BOM below standard multiplex allowance.

title: Requisition 3C — Split mast sequencing
subject: DIRECTIVE 84-C: HEAVY TRUCK CORRIDOR
body: Westbound heavy-class envelopes are tripping kinetic overlap in shared E/W slices. Implement split-bank sequencing: one full relay slice services W_LEFT, W_STRAIGHT, and W_RIGHT; a separate slice services the east mast. No simultaneous hard .GO on opposing E/W vectors in the same macro-slice.
bullets: Discharge quota: 250 units from sector tally.
West mast discharges in isolation from east mast.
Concurrent E/W hard .GO on the same macro-slice is not indemnified.
bureauMemo: West mast heavy envelopes flagged; split-bank evidence must show zero simultaneous opposing E/W hard .GO within one macro-slice.

title: Requisition 4A — Eight-bank multiplex
subject: DIRECTIVE 85-A: 8-PHASE CHALLENGE
body: All twelve vehicular vectors are strapped live; ingress indices are pegged high across compasses. Author an eight-bank rotation that keeps the conflict matrix satisfied and ILC buffers below the published ERROR_0x94 halt depth. Thermal shunt (ERROR_0xAF) is one relay budget mistake away.
bullets: Discharge quota: 500 units from sector tally.
Service every strapped vehicular movement over the audit window.
Buffer saturation (ERROR_0x94) trips when any single approach buffer exceeds the halt depth in §1.4.
bureauMemo: Eight-bank multiplex is capital-grade; thermal shunt (ERROR_0xAF) voids warranty if green-sum exceeds synchronization envelope even once.

title: Requisition 4B — Priority comparator corridor
subject: DIRECTIVE 85-B: HOSPITAL ACCESS
body: Southbound strap is designated EMS ingress. ILC-92 returns on SOUTH_STRAIGHT shall not dwell in the red band: use comparator-driven phase_insert to purge southbound depth the moment the coprocessor return exceeds two units. Bureau memo caps sustained southbound queue display at five units for this docket.
bullets: Discharge quota: 400 units from sector tally.
Primary audit vector: SOUTH_STRAIGHT discharge continuity.
Keep SOUTH_STRAIGHT ILC return below five units under nominal demand.
bureauMemo: EMS strap memo: southbound queue display capped at five units nominal; sustained overrun opens Hospital Liaison ticket HL-22.

title: SVE — off-grid validation rack
subject: SEC-082 SVE / UNSCORED PARTITION
body: Simulated Validation Environment partition mounted. All ILC-92 heads and relay shelves are provisioned without procurement caps. Burn phase-sequence images here before filing scored corridor dockets; Bureau ledger does not ingest SVE logs.
bullets: No mandated discharge quota.
Adjust demand curves and logic images manually.
bureauMemo: SVE partition carries no procurement ceiling; scored corridor burns must still file on hardware BOM before audit upload.
